name: Smoked Turkey Gumbo
recipeIngredient: 1 Smoked turkey carcass, including any skin
1 Sprig Fresh Thyme or 1/4 teaspoon Dried Thyme
1/2 cup Finely Diced Green or Red Bell Pepper
1 tsp Chile Powder (New Mexico, California, or Ancho)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the carcass in a stock pot and break it up if necessary to make it fit. Add water to cover and bring to a boil. Reduce to a simmer and skim off any foam that comes to the surface.
2. Add the sliced onion, celery, parsley, thyme sprig, and bay leaf; simmer uncovered, skimming occasionally for 2 to 4 hours. The longer you cook it, the richer the stock will be. Do not let the stock boil or it will turn cloudy.
3. Heat the oil in a large, heavy skillet (preferably cast iron) over medium heat. Stir in the flour and cook, stirring and scraping the entire bottom of the pan at least every 10 seconds until the roux cooks to a deep mahogany color, 10 to 15 minutes. Adjust the heat to prevent scorching.
4. Turn off the heat and continue stirring for a minute or two until the mixture no longer gets darker on the bottom.
5. Carefully stir in the diced vegetables; do not let the roux splatter as it can cause burns. Stir to coat the vegetables evenly with roux, then stir in the chile powder, salt, pepper, herbs, and cayenne. Set aside. The roux may be prepared to this point while the stock cooks.
6. Strain the stock through a fine-mesh sieve into a large pot, let it stand until the fat rises to the top, and skim off the fat.
7. Bring 8 cups of the strained stock to a boil in a large pot. Stir in the roux vegetable mixture and simmer until slightly thickened, about 15 minutes.
8. Add the shrimp, crab, and/or oysters and simmer just until done, about 5 minutes. Taste for seasoning and correct if necessary. Serve hot over small scoops of rice.
Serves 6 to 8.
